![ICON House Zero Exterior a Photo Credit Casey Dunn](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D%27http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg%27%20width%3D%271%27%20height%3D%271%27%20style%3D%27background%3Atransparent%27%2F%3E)

# House Zero

**3D-printed Home Pushes Boundaries of Resilient and Sustainable Architecture and Design**

House Zero is a 2,000+ sq-ft, 3 bedroom/2.5 bath home with a 350 sq-ft, 1 bedroom/1 bath accessory dwelling unit, featuring ICON’s resilient 3D-printed wall system, which replaces a building system traditionally made up of multiple steps saving time, waste and cost. The walls of the home are made with a proprietary cementitious-based material, “Lavacrete,” insulation, and steel for reinforcing; they were printed in less than two weeks.

Located in East Austin, “House Zero,” was 3D printed by ICON’s next-gen Vulcan construction system and designed by Lake|Flato, known for their sustainable designs. Blending mid-century modernist ranch house aesthetics, the home features an elevated architectural and energy efficient design that highlights the benefits of resiliency and sustainability, with the digital possibilities of additive construction.

Biophilic principles—the way that humans connect with nature and nature-inspired compositions —are behind much of “House Zero’s” design and best accomplished using ICON’s next-generation 3D-printing technology. The curved walls create structurally efficient self-stability, while rounded corners allow for softer, more naturalistic circulation routes throughout the home. Doorways and windows were strategically placed to accentuate accompanying landscapes; eastern-facing high clerestory windows illuminate the living room with soft, yet dynamic sunlight, minimizing the need for electrical lighting throughout the day.

![House Zero Living Room](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D%27http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg%27%20viewBox%3D%270%200%201%2011%27%2F%3E)

“Houses like this are only possible with 3D printing, and this is the new standard of what 3D printing can mean for the world,” said ICON CEO and co-founder Jason Ballard. “My hope is that this home will provoke architects, developers, builders, and homeowners to dream alongside ICON about the exciting and hopeful future that robotic construction, and specifically 3D printing, makes possible.”

Since its [unveiling](https://www.iconbuild.com/newsroom/icon-unveils-house-zero-and-announces-2022-sxsw-activations), House Zero has won multiple awards in the architecture and design communities, including being named one of [TIME's Best Inventions of 2022](https://www.iconbuild.com/newsroom/icons-house-zero-time-best-inventions-2022), Builder Magazine’s Project of the Year for the 2022 Builder’s Choice Design Awards, an Innovation By Design award from Fast Company, a Texas Society of Architects TxA Design Award and two (2) [Architizer A+ Awards](https://www.iconbuild.com/newsroom/icons-house-zero-receives-top-honors-in-architizers-annual-a-awards).

# House Zero

*“The combination of traditional and advanced technologies creates a contrast that is clear yet balanced in this innovative home that is very much of its time.”  
— Paul Danna, FAIA*

**Location** Austin  
**Client/Contractor** ICON  
**Architect** Lake|Flato Architects  
**Design Team** Lewis McNeel, AIA, Ashley Heeren, AIA, Margaux Palmer, Vicki Yuan, AIA, Nyssa Sherazee, AIA, Cole Major, AIA, Warren Weaver  
**Structural Engineer** Guy Nordenson and Associates  
**MEP Engineer** Integral Group  
**Lighting Design** Studio Lumina  
**Envelope Consultant** SGH  
**Material Science Consultant** WJE  
**Landscape Architect** Word + Carr

House Zero is a field trial for a proprietary concrete wall printing system; it’s also a new climate-responsive home. The design team collaborated with software developers, robotics engineers, and material scientists to create a new set of architectural strategies for printed concrete construction. While the house is made using robotic printing processes, it showcases biophilic design principles throughout, including the use of natural wood, plentiful daylighting, and connections to nature. Designed for net-zero energy, it features a thermally broken, insulated envelope and a rapid software-controlled construction process, pushing the current limits of additive manufacturing at the scale of a building.

--- SOURCE 3: House Zero - Lake Flato ---
URL: https://www.lakeflato.com/project/house-zero/

SUMMARY:
Natural wood, plentiful daylighting and views to nature provide a timeless and rooted-to-the-earth quality, drawing attention to the natural world through

FULL CONTENT:
![]()

# House Zero

House Zero is a demonstration project and field trial for ICON’s proprietary concrete wall printing system. More than that, House Zero is also a compelling and climate-responsive new home connecting its inhabitants to a native Texas landscape and an evolving Austin neighborhood fabric. Built for permanence and resilience, the plan allows for the flexibility of ever-evolving patterns of living and aging-in-place that a family experiences over the course of a lifetime.

In collaboration with ICON.

![]()

Austin, TX

[Residential](https://www.lakeflato.com/project_type/residential/)

[Architecture](https://www.lakeflato.com/service/architecture/), [Interiors](https://www.lakeflato.com/service/interiors/)

[Urban](https://www.lakeflato.com/geography/urban/)

[Net Zero](https://www.lakeflato.com/innovation/net-zero/)

3,000 sf

![]()

The house expresses its construction materials proudly: the concrete walls are framed and protected by simple ordered wood elements. Automation has been considered in all aspects of the project, which utilizes prefabrication of structural members and interior components to maximize speed and efficiency in construction. Natural wood, plentiful daylighting and views to nature provide a timeless and rooted-to-the-earth quality, drawing attention to the natural world through biophilic design principles.

![]()
![]()
![]()

In keeping with Lake Flato’s longstanding explorations of buildings and craft, this is a house distilled down to essentials. House Zero uses new technology to display enduring things: the honest effort and the raw materials needed to provide lasting shelter, and a deep-rooted sense of place and home.

![]()
![]()
![]()

## Design for Innovation

Lake Flato collaborated intensely with ICON’s software developers, robotics engineers, and material scientists to create a new set of architectural innovations and strategies for printed concrete construction. The LF team then put these innovations to use designing a welcoming, practical home that is desirable, livable, and that expands the performance capabilities of 3D printing technology. Designed for net-zero energy, the house features a thermally broken, robustly insulated envelope and a rapid software-controlled construction process, showcasing and pushing the current limits of additive manufacturing at the scale of a building.

![]()

--- SOURCE 4: House Zero - 3D-printed Home Pushes Boundaries of Resilient and ... ---
URL: https://homeworlddesign.com/house-zero-3d-printed-home/

SUMMARY:
Designed for net-zero energy, the house features a thermally broken, robustly insulated envelope and a rapid software-controlled

FULL CONTENT:
![HomeWorldDesign](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/cropped-logo-v33-1276-250.webp)
![HomeWorldDesign](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/cropped-logo-v33-1276-250.webp)

# House Zero – 3D-printed Home Pushes Boundaries of Resilient and Sustainable Architecture and Design

![House Zero - 3D-printed Home](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-3-70.jpg)

![House Zero - 3D-printed Home](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-3-70.jpg)

Project: House Zero  
Architects: [Lake Flato Architects](https://homeworlddesign.com/office/lake-flato-architects/)  
Builder: [ICON](https://www.iconbuild.com/)  
Location: Austin, [Texas](https://homeworlddesign.com/bellaire-residence-lake-flato-architects/), United States  
Completed 2022  
Photo Credits: [Casey Dunn Photography](https://homeworlddesign.com/tag/casey-dunn-photography/)

House Zero is a demonstration project and field trial for ICON’s proprietary concrete wall printing system. More than that, House Zero is also a compelling and climate-responsive new home connecting its inhabitants to a native Texas landscape and an evolving Austin neighborhood fabric. Built for permanence and resilience, the plan allows for the flexibility of ever-evolving patterns of living and aging-in-place that a family experiences over the course of a lifetime.

![House Zero - 3D-printed Home](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-4-62.jpg)

![House Zero - 3D-printed Home](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-4-62.jpg)

[Lake|Flato](https://www.lakeflato.com/) collaborated intensely with ICON’s software developers, robotics engineers, and material scientists to create a new set of architectural innovations and strategies for printed concrete construction. The LF team then put these innovations to use designing a welcoming, practical home that is desirable, livable, and that expands the performance capabilities of [3D printing technology](https://homeworlddesign.com/3d-printing-is-transforming-the-construction-industry/). Designed for net-zero energy, the house features a thermally broken, robustly insulated envelope and a rapid software-controlled construction process, showcasing and pushing the current limits of additive manufacturing at the scale of a building.

![outdoor, Lake Flato Architects](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201000%20666'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![outdoor, Lake Flato Architects](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-2-66.jpg)

![outdoor, Lake Flato Architects](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201000%20666'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![outdoor, Lake Flato Architects](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-2-66.jpg)

The house expresses its construction materials proudly: the concrete walls are framed and protected by simple ordered wood elements. Although the house is made using new robotic printing processes, its natural wood, plentiful daylighting and views to nature provide a timeless and rooted-to-the-earth quality, drawing attention to the natural world through [biophilic design principles](https://homeworlddesign.com/the-low-effort-low-cost-biophilic-design-guide/).

![living room, Lake Flato Architects](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201000%20758'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![living room, Lake Flato Architects](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-8-60.jpg)

![living room, Lake Flato Architects](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201000%20758'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![living room, Lake Flato Architects](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-8-60.jpg)

In keeping with Lake|Flato’s longstanding explorations of buildings and craft, this is a house distilled down to essentials. House Zero uses new technology to display enduring things: the honest effort and the raw materials needed to provide lasting shelter, and a deep-rooted sense of place and home.

![entry hall, Lake Flato Architects](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201000%201303'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![entry hall, Lake Flato Architects](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-9-51.jpg)

![entry hall, Lake Flato Architects](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201000%201303'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![entry hall, Lake Flato Architects](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-9-51.jpg)

“House Zero is ground zero for the emergence of entirely new design languages and architectural vernaculars that will use robotic construction to deliver the things we need most from our housing: comfort, beauty, dignity, sustainability, attainability, and hope. Houses like this are only possible with 3D printing, and this is the new standard of what 3D printing can mean for the world. My hope is that this home will provoke architects, developers, builders, and homeowners to dream alongside ICON about the exciting and hopeful future that robotic construction, and specifically 3D printing, makes possible. The housing of our future must be different from the housing we have known.” Jason Ballard / ICON, Co-Founder and CEO

![bedroom, Lake Flato Architects](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201000%201292'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![bedroom, Lake Flato Architects](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-10-49.jpg)

![bedroom, Lake Flato Architects](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201000%201292'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![bedroom, Lake Flato Architects](https://f7e5m2b4.delivery.rocketcdn.me/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/1-10-49.jpg)

# ICON and Lake|Flato Architects unveil 3D printed 'House Zero' in Austin, Texas

by [Jerry Elengical](https://www.stirworld.com/author-jerry-elengical "Jerry Elengical")Published on : Mar 11, 2022

Described as the first of a new generation of homes that harness the benefits of [3D printing](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-3d-printing) to their fullest, 'House Zero' by [ICON](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-icon) and [Texas](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-texas)-based firm Lake|Flato Architects has been presented as a prototype for sustainable, energy efficient homes in the future that will be assembled over far shorter timelines than their present-day counterparts. In support of this latest venture into utilising 3D printing to tackle the challenge of providing [affordable housing](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-affordable-housing), [US](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-united-states)-based company ICON - a leader in the emerging realm of [3D printed architecture](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-3d-printed-architecture), has developed yet another groundbreaking case for this revolutionary technology in [Austin](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-austin), Texas. To a degree, this endeavour translates elements of the organisation’s earlier explorations into [extraterrestrial architecture](https://www.stirworld.com/see-features-to-the-moon-mars-and-beyond-innovative-space-architecture-projects-from-2021) alongside [Bjarke Ingels Group](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-bjarke-ingels-group) (such as [Project Olympus](https://www.stirworld.com/see-features-icon-big-and-search-reveal-project-olympus-as-3d-printed-homes-on-moon) and [Mars Dune Alpha](https://www.stirworld.com/see-news-big-and-icon-unveil-mars-dune-alpha-a-3d-printed-habitat-for-the-red-planet)), into a liveable reality back on Earth.

![The residence is the first project in ICON’s ‘Exploration’ series, 3D printed using the company’s ‘Vulcan’ construction system | House Zero | ICON and Lake|Flato Architects | STIRworld](images/see/3203_ICON_1.jpg)

"House Zero is ground zero for the emergence of entirely new design languages and architectural vernaculars that will use [robotic](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-robotics) construction to deliver the things we need most from our housing: comfort, beauty, dignity, sustainability, attainability, and hope,” notes Jason Ballard, co-founder and CEO of ICON, in an official release. He adds, “Houses like this are only possible with 3D printing, and this is the new standard of what 3D printing can mean for the world.” As the first project in their 'Exploration' Series, House Zero was 3D printed using ICON’s cutting-edge 'Vulcan' construction system, with walls composed of 'Lavacrete' - a proprietary cementitious material developed by the organisation for their 3D printing applications.

![Assembled with ‘Lavacrete’ - a proprietary cementitious material developed by ICON, the structure’s curved walls feature furrowed textures | House Zero | ICON and Lake|Flato Architects | STIRworld](images/see/3203_ICON_1.jpg)

Additive construction methods enabled the realisation of the mid-century [modernist](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-modernism) ranch theme that pervades the [residential design](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-residential-design). Over more than 2,000 sq ft of floor area, House Zero accommodates three bedrooms, 2.5 bathrooms, as well as a 350 sq ft single bedroom and bathroom accessory dwelling unit. The 3D printed wall system used in assembling the home is said to have significantly shortened construction durations and minimised material wastage as well as costs when pitted against traditional construction methods that require multiple stages for completion. Furthermore, the Lavacrete and reinforced steel wall assembly is said to have sufficient thermal mass to mitigate heat transfer when paired with insulation and an airtight configuration that optimises energy efficiency and life-cycle costs.

![ICON’s ‘Vulcan’ construction system is a next generation robotic 3D printing mechanism | House Zero | ICON and Lake|Flato Architects | STIRworld](images/see/3203_ICON_3.jpg)

Furrowed [concrete architectural](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-concrete-architecture) masses are complemented by wooden members and canopies in the [façade design](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-facade-design), which remains understated in its articulation, relying on the play of textures and proportions to speak first. "While the organic nature of the 3D-printed concrete and curved walls are new design languages for us, House Zero was still entirely in line with the natural connections we seek in our architecture," says Ashley Heeren, an Associate at Lake|Flato, in a press release. “The home expresses our shared passions for craft and performance in an inviting and comfortable family home constructed through a totally new way of building. It’s been a thrill for our team to work with ICON on such an innovative home design and be a part of the future of homebuilding."

![The system is said to significantly reduce construction times due to the lower number of phases it involves | House Zero | ICON and Lake|Flato Architects | STIRworld](images/see/3203_ICON_4.jpg)
![ICON hopes to pave the way for a new approach towards providing sustainable and affordable housing to the masses | House Zero | ICON and Lake|Flato Architects | STIRworld](images/see/3203_ICON_5.jpg)

Lake|Flato Architects channelled elements of [biophilic design](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-biophilic-design) and forms inspired by nature into the curved walls used in the home, which were 3D printed using the Vulcan construction system. As per the architects, the curved profiles of these walls impart greater structural stability to the ensemble, and also create softer circulation routes as part of the [interior design](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-interior-design). "The 3D-printed wall design and its inherent biophilic quality conveys an ordered, yet non-rigid pattern invoking a rooted and timeless sense of natural refuge,” relays Lewis McNeel, an Associate Partner at Lake|Flato Architects, in a press statement.

![Curved walls create smooth circulation routes through the interior | House Zero | ICON and Lake|Flato Architects | STIRworld](images/see/3203_ICON_6.jpg)

Careful considerations were also made in the placement and design of openings. For instance, high clerestory windows located along the home’s eastern face have been incorporated to allow for natural illumination in the living room during daytime, reducing the need for artificial light. Hence, despite the revolutionary new reimagination of homebuilding embodied within this project, the [residence’s architecture](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-residential-architecture) does not lose sight of the essential tenets of this art form. Jason Ballard shares, “My hope is that this home will provoke architects, developers, builders, and homeowners to dream alongside ICON about the exciting and hopeful future that robotic construction, and specifically 3D printing, makes possible. The [housing](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-housing) of our future must be different from the housing we have known.”

![The interior design channels the aesthetic of mid-century modern ranch home | House Zero | ICON and Lake|Flato Architects | STIRworld](images/see/3203_ICON_7.jpg)

In its role as an official partner for the South by Southwest Festival 2022 (SXSW) occurring from March 11-20 in Austin, Texas, ICON will also be conducting guided tours of the residence between March 13-14. Additionally, the company’s CEO and Founder Jason Ballard will also deliver a featured talk at the event on March 15, titled *It’s Time to Build: a Conversation with Jason Ballard*. During the session, Ballard will discuss the prospective role of architecture, material technology, and robotics in addressing many of the human race's most pressing challenges including affordable housing, community health, [sustainability](https://www.stirworld.com/tags-sustainability), climate resiliency, and the advancement of human potential.

![ICON will also be conducting tours of the home from 13-14 March during SXSW 2022 in Austin, Texas | House Zero | ICON and Lake|Flato Architects | STIRworld](images/see/3203_ICON_8.jpg)

# ICON and Lake|Flato Architects unveil 3D printed 'House Zero' in Austin, Texas

### Featuring curving, furrowed concrete walls 3D printed using ICON’s 'Vulcan' construction system, the revolutionary home will be open for tours from March 13-14 during SXSW 2022.

by [Jerry Elengical](https://www.stirworld.com/author-jerry-elengical "Jerry Elengical") | Published on : Mar 11, 2022

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://images.dwell.com/photos/6711721526265430016/6904256626491793408/large.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The house features a modern design with large cylindrical concrete structures, extensive use of wood, and biophilic elements integrated into the architecture.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://i.pinimg.com/originals/bd/51/2a/bd512a4e123deff8c91577e06ad3362f.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The house features biophilic design with curved, textured concrete walls, wooden beams supporting a flat roof, and abundant greenery integrated into the exterior.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://blog.zeelproject.com/uploads/posts/2022-03-10/1646894700_2222.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A living room with a warm, natural aesthetic features a wooden ceiling and wall panels, a leather sofa, a modern armchair, and botanical elements integrated into the biophilic design.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://assets.newatlas.com/dims4/default/abf09ec/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2063x1375+438+0/resize/840x560!/quality/90/?url=http://newatlas-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com/3e/d1/949fc865451e95b66c77472532f9/icon-housezero-conceptrender-image-credit-lakeflatoarchitects.png
DESCRIPTION: The house features a modern, biophilic design with curving walls, extensive use of natural materials, and large overhangs, integrated with surrounding landscaping and plants to create a harmonious connection between indoor and outdoor environments.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://i.pinimg.com/originals/e7/01/70/e70170c1ad396d01a36b2d8e1f98068e.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A modern house featuring biophilic design elements with curved concrete walls, large wooden overhangs, and an engaging connection to natural landscape through greenery and potted plants.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: House Zero incorporates biophilic design principles, emphasizing the connection between inhabitants and nature through natural materials, ample daylighting, and strategically placed windows that enhance views of the landscape.
Utilizing ICON's Vulcan construction system, House Zero was 3D printed in under two weeks, significantly reducing construction time and material waste compared to traditional building methods.
The home showcases innovative architectural strategies with curved walls for structural stability, advanced insulation for energy efficiency, and a design that adapts to evolving living patterns.
